patch.value: pm install should accept multiple package targets (help already advertises [targets...])

2026-07-06 audit #12 sandbox: pm install pm-a pm-b errors 'Install accepts one package source at a time' although help/usage shows install [options] [targets...]. Either loop installs over each target sequentially (preferred agent UX, saves N-1 process spawns) or fix the advertised signature. Wildcard '*' already batch-installs bundled packages, so batch semantics exist.
pm install a b c installs all three (per-target result rows, partial-failure reporting) or usage/help no longer advertises variadic targets; spec covers multi-target path.

author: codex-cli-agent
message: Start multi-target install implementation in the agent-UX package install bundle.

value.author: codex-cli-agent
value.text: Evidence: implemented sequential multi-target package install aggregation with per-target rows, warning de-duplication, and partial-failure exit code handling. Verified focused specs, full coverage 100/100/100/100, typecheck, lint, audit, and installed-tarball temp dogfood.

patch.value: Implemented multi-target package install with per-target aggregation, partial-failure reporting, warning de-duplication, focused tests, full 100% coverage, type/lint/audit gates, and installed-tarball dogfood evidence.
pm install accepts multiple explicit targets and reports per-target success or failure without losing wildcard install behavior.
Multi-target install now runs targets sequentially, emits aggregate JSON rows with installed/failed counts, preserves wildcard collapse, and sets a failure exit code when any target fails.
multi-target install aggregation and partial-failure reporting
ESLint suppression budget ratchet lowered after lint prune

value.note: multi-target install success,partial failure,wildcard regression coverage

value.author: codex-cli-agent
value.text: Evidence update: release gate found the pruned ESLint suppression baseline needed its static-quality budget lowered; updated MAX_ESLINT_SUPPRESSIONS 108 -> 105 and verified release-automation-contract spec passes.

value.author: codex-cli-agent
value.text: Review response: accepted Greptile P2 on multi-target install success accounting. Changed targetOk to require result.ok === true so omitted ok is counted as failure, matching aggregate failure detection. Verification: register-commands focused suite and pnpm typecheck pass.

value.author: codex-cli-agent
value.text: Review response: accepted Sourcery/Gemini feedback on aggregate install robustness. Multi-target install now preserves the first specific failure exit code instead of always using generic failure, and the result path tolerates missing warning arrays without unsafe spreading. Verification: node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/commands/register-commands.spec.ts tests/unit/extensions/extension-command.spec.ts passed (197 tests); pnpm typecheck passed.

value.author: codex-cli-agent
value.text: Review response: accepted Greptile P2 on aggregate install exit-code precedence. A soft ok:false row still contributes a generic failure fallback, but a later specific PmCliError exit code can now replace that generic fallback so exit-code-only consumers get the more actionable code. Verification: focused register/extension tests passed (197 tests); pnpm typecheck passed.

value.author: codex-cli-agent
value.text: Evidence update: addressed review feedback for multi-target package install. serializeExtensionInstallError now preserves full PmCliError context via flat object spread, and multi-target install rejects --gh/--github before dispatch with code multi_target_github_install_ambiguous. Focused register test passed; full coverage passed at 100/100/100/100 across 5446 tests; isolated temp dogfood verified both --github and --gh guards.

value.author: codex-cli-agent
value.text: Review response: accepted committed-head Greptile P2 on soft-fail install rows. Multi-target install rows for runExtension results with ok:false now include a minimal error payload with message, exit_code, and code=extension_install_soft_failed, so agent consumers do not receive a silent {target, ok:false} shape. The static-import Greptile note was not applied because repo policy for this task forbids inline/dynamic imports. Verification: focused register/extension tests passed (198 tests), pnpm typecheck passed, and full coverage passed at 100/100/100/100 across 5446 tests.

value.author: codex-cli-agent
value.text: Review evidence: Greptile review d87bab0e-c4a1-480e-b838-de28b506359e was run after local amend but before pushing the branch. It repeated the soft-fail diagnostics finding, while local committed head 49e9ff84 already adds extension_install_soft_failed rows and focused tests cover the shape. The static-import startup-cost finding remains intentionally rejected for this lane because the repo-wide code-quality rule forbids inline/dynamic imports; rerun Greptile after pushing the amended branch so remote review sees the current head.

value.author: codex-cli-agent
value.text: Evidence update: after pushing e0798619, Sentry/telemetry gate passed with Sentry project unbrained/pm-cli checked over 14 days, critical=0, high=0, telemetry finish error rate 0.85%, and failures_without_error_code_rows=0. Review follow-up: accepted Cubic tracker metadata finding on pm-7cx8 and updated closed-item resolution/expected_result/actual_result/acceptance_criteria through pm update; Gemini static-import suggestion remains intentionally rejected because this task's repo-wide quality rules forbid inline/dynamic imports.

value.author: codex-cli-agent
value.text: Review response update: accepted current-head CodeRabbit feedback. Compacted the 41 Semgrep import history streams with pm history-compact --ids ... --min-entries 1 to remove no-op history_redact markers while preserving replay integrity; verified no local Semgrep CSV download path remains in .agents/pm and pm validate --check-resolution --check-history-drift stays ok. Also accepted the low-value extension test fixture dedupe by extracting withWidgetPackageRoot for the two neighboring package-root tests. Verification: focused extension-command spec passed (116 tests), pnpm typecheck passed, pnpm lint passed with jscpd 0 clones/static gate passed, pnpm security:scan passed, git diff --check passed.
